preamble: The shift from 'tʲ' to 'ʃ' is a case of spirantization where a palatalized dental stop simplifies into a postalveolar fricative. This change often occurs via an intermediate affricate stage [tʃ].

examples.note: Dialectal variation where palatalized dental stops simplify to postalveolar fricatives.
In certain dialects, /t/ before /i/ becomes [tʃ] and can simplify further.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Schenker, A. M. (1995). The Dawn of Slavic.
